homepage.heroSubline: Legacy Plumbing has been keeping Atlanta homes flowing smoothly for over a decade. We understand how Georgia's climate and aging pipes affect your home.
homepage.aboutTitle: Rooted in Atlanta, Built on Trust
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Legacy Plumbing began as a small family operation serving historic neighborhoods like Virginia-Highland and Inman Park. We've grown by treating every home like it's our own.
Our approach combines old-school craftsmanship with modern solutions tailored to Atlanta's unique plumbing challenges. We know red clay soil shifts pipes and summer humidity accelerates corrosion.
When we're not under your sink, we're volunteering with Atlanta Habitat for Humanity or sponsoring Little League teams across Cobb County. This city's our home too.

valueProps.title: Local Knowledge
valueProps.description: We've serviced every type of Atlanta home from Craftsman bungalows to modern infill houses, understanding their unique plumbing needs.

valueProps.title: Preventative Focus
valueProps.description: Our maintenance plans help avoid costly repairs from Georgia's extreme weather and soil conditions.

valueProps.title: Neighborly Service
valueProps.description: Same friendly technicians every visit who remember your home's plumbing history.

valueProps.title: No Surprises
valueProps.description: Upfront pricing with detailed explanations before we turn a wrench.

homepage.ctaSubline: Our Atlanta-based team is ready to help with any plumbing need, big or small.

services.name: Emergency Plumbing
services.shortDescription: 24/7 response when Atlanta's weather or aging pipes cause crises.
services.description:
When a pipe bursts during an Atlanta ice storm or your basement floods after a summer thunderstorm, we're here day or night.

Our emergency vans are stocked with parts common in Georgia homes - from galvanized pipe fittings for older bungalows to PEX supplies for modern renovations. We prioritize stopping active water damage first, then provide transparent repair options.

Common emergency calls include: frozen pipe bursts in Buckhead's historic homes, sewer backups from root intrusion in Midtown's mature trees, and water heater failures during pollen season when sediment clogs systems.
process: Step 1: Immediate phone assessment to contain damage
Step 2: Arrival within 90 minutes with full diagnostic equipment
Step 3: Temporary solution implemented if complex repair needed
Step 4: Detailed follow-up report with prevention recommendations
benefits: Georgia-licensed master plumbers on every truck

faq.question: How quickly can you respond to emergencies?
faq.answer: We guarantee a technician will be on the road within 30 minutes of your call, typically arriving in under 90 minutes across metro Atlanta. Our Marietta and Roswell locations help us cover the northern suburbs quickly.

faq.question: Do you handle sewer line emergencies?
faq.answer: Yes, we're equipped for sewer scopes and repairs, especially common in neighborhoods with older clay pipes like those found in Druid Hills and Candler Park.

services.name: Water Heater Services
services.shortDescription: Installation and repair for Atlanta's hard water conditions.
services.description:
Georgia's mineral-rich water shortens water heater lifespans, especially in homes built before 2000. We specialize in solutions for Atlanta's unique conditions.

For historic homes, we retrofit compatible tankless systems that fit in tight spaces. In newer subdivisions, we install hybrid heat pump models that handle summer humidity efficiently. Every installation includes whole-house water pressure testing - crucial with our area's fluctuating municipal supply.

Signs you need service: rusty water in Old Fourth Ward apartments, rumbling noises in Vinings townhomes, or lukewarm showers in Sandy Springs during peak usage times.
process: Step 1: Water quality and usage assessment
Step 2: Energy-efficient model recommendations
Step 3: Permitted installation with flood prevention
Step 4: Post-installation pressure adjustment
benefits: Tankless options for small Atlanta basements

faq.question: How often should Atlanta water heaters be serviced?
faq.Answer: Annual maintenance is crucial here - sediment builds up faster with our water hardness. We recommend flushing before pollen season when mineral deposits combine with sediment.

services.name: Drain Cleaning
services.shortDescription: Solving Atlanta's common root intrusion and grease clog issues.
services.description:
Between Southern cooking traditions and our lush tree canopy, Atlanta homes face unique drain challenges. We use camera inspections to pinpoint problems without unnecessary digging.

In older neighborhoods like Grant Park, we frequently find terra cotta pipes compromised by magnolia roots. In newer areas like Brookhaven, improper grease disposal causes kitchen line blockages. Our hydro-jetting trucks can handle even the toughest clogs in Atlanta's variable pipe materials.

Watch for these signs: Multiple fixtures backing up in Decatur bungalows, gurgling sounds in Smyrna bathroom drains, or slow drainage in Dunwoody kitchen sinks after holiday meals.
process: Step 1: Camera inspection to diagnose blockage type
Step 2: Method selection (snaking, hydro-jetting, etc.)
Step 3: Root treatment for prevention if needed
Step 4: Disposal recommendations to prevent recurrence
benefits: Service records for future home sales

faq.question: Can you handle main sewer line clogs?
faq.Answer: Absolutely. We specialize in clearing Atlanta's common 4-6" clay and cast iron main lines, using techniques that won't damage older pipes found in communities like East Lake and Kirkwood.

services.name: Pipe Repair & Replacement
services.shortDescription: Solutions for Atlanta's shifting soil and aging pipe systems.
services.description:
Georgia's expansive red clay causes more pipe movement than most regions, leading to leaks at joints and stress fractures. We've mastered repair techniques for every era of Atlanta housing.

For historic homes in Cabbagetown, we preserve original plumbing where possible using sleeve repairs. In 1980s subdivisions like those around Perimeter Mall, we replace failing polybutylene pipes before catastrophic failures occur. Our trenchless methods minimize disruption to your landscaping and hardscapes.

Warning signs include: Unexplained water bills in Ansley Park homes, damp spots in Buckhead crawl spaces, or reduced pressure in Chamblee's mid-century ranches.
process: Step 1: Pressure testing and camera inspection
Step 2: Material analysis (galvanized, copper, etc.)
Step 3: Repair vs. replacement recommendation
Step 4: Permitted work with city inspection coordination

faq.question: How do I know if my pipes need replacement?
faq.Answer: Frequent pinhole leaks, discolored water, and pressure drops suggest systemic issues - common in Atlanta homes built between 1960-1990. We'll show you camera footage to make informed decisions.

services.name: Bathroom Remodeling
services.shortDescription: Plumbing upgrades for Atlanta's bathroom renovations.
services.description:
Atlanta's mix of historic charm and modern living creates unique bathroom plumbing challenges. We help homeowners preserve character while adding functionality.

In Virginia-Highland bungalows, we carefully relocate plumbing in pier-and-beam foundations. For high-rise condos in Midtown, we coordinate with building management on riser access. Every project begins with a water efficiency assessment - crucial with our summer drought risks.

Popular upgrades include: Converting clawfoot tubs to walk-in showers in Inman Park, adding laundry hookups to O4W lofts, and creating spa bathrooms in Buckhead estates without compromising water pressure.
process: Step 1: Structural assessment for older homes
Step 2: Fixture and layout planning
Step 3: Permitting assistance
Step 4: Precision installation with daily cleanup

faq.question: Can you work with my historic home's limitations?
faq.Answer: Absolutely. We've successfully updated plumbing in Atlanta Landmark homes throughout Druid Hills and Morningside without compromising their character or violating preservation guidelines.

services.name: Leak Detection
services.shortDescription: Finding hidden leaks before Atlanta's soil shifts cause damage.
services.description:
Undetected leaks in Atlanta homes can lead to foundation issues and mold growth in our humid climate. Our advanced detection methods pinpoint problems without unnecessary wall demolition.

Using thermal imaging and acoustic sensors, we trace leaks common in Georgia homes: slab leaks in ranch homes from soil movement, condensation issues in high-rise condos, and supply line drips behind the brick walls of historic properties. We provide detailed moisture maps to guide repairs.

Be alert for: Musty smells in Sandy Springs basements, warm spots on hardwood floors in Decatur, or unexplained vegetation growth near foundations in Vinings.
process: Step 1: Non-invasive moisture mapping
Step 2: Pressure testing of systems
Step 3: Thermal and acoustic diagnostics
Step 4: Repair plan with moisture remediation

faq.question: How accurate are your leak detection methods?
faq.Answer: Our equipment can locate leaks within 6 inches - crucial for Atlanta homes where a small leak can travel far in our porous red clay before surfacing.

reviews.name: Marcus T.
reviews.text: When a pipe burst in our 1930s Ansley Park home during last January's freeze, Legacy had a team here in 45 minutes. They not only fixed the immediate issue but showed me where other vulnerable spots were in our old plumbing system. Six months later, their maintenance plan caught another weak joint before it failed.

reviews.name: Elena R.
reviews.text: Our Dunwoody home's water heater died right before family arrived for Thanksgiving. Legacy installed a new heat pump model that handles our hard water beautifully. The technician even adjusted our whole-house pressure to prevent stress on the old pipes upstairs. Five months later, our energy bills are noticeably lower.

reviews.name: James and Laurie K.
reviews.text: We wanted to update our Virginia-Highland bungalow's bathroom without losing its charm. Legacy designed plumbing that accommodated the original tile work while adding modern water efficiency. They coordinated perfectly with our carpenter and preserved the heart pine floors we'd worried about protecting.

reviews.name: Nina P.
reviews.text: After three plumbers couldn't solve our recurring drain issues in our Brookhaven home, Legacy's camera found roots growing through a poorly sealed joint. Their hydro-jetting cleared it completely, and the root inhibitor treatment has kept it clear through Atlanta's rainy spring. Finally - a permanent solution!

reviews.name: David W.
reviews.text: The leak detection service saved us thousands. Legacy found moisture under our Buckhead home's foundation that six other inspections missed. Their trenchless pipe replacement minimized disruption to our landscaping and was completed in one day. The detailed report helped with our insurance claim too.

reviews.name: Sophia M.
reviews.text: Our Inman Park loft had mysterious water stains appearing on the ceiling. Legacy's thermal imaging found a condensation issue between floors that our building's maintenance team had overlooked. Their repair was clean and efficient, and they followed up twice to ensure everything stayed dry during Atlanta's humid summer.

service-areas.title: Atlanta Plumbing Service Areas | Legacy
service-areas.description: We provide complete plumbing services to homes across Atlanta including Marietta, Roswell, Alpharetta, Johns Creek and Kennesaw.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Plumbing proudly serves Marietta, GA, a vibrant city with a population of around 60,000 residents. Many homes in Marietta were built in the 1960s and 1970s, featuring classic suburban subdivisions that often require plumbing updates or repairs due to aging systems. Our team is experienced in handling the unique challenges posed by older plumbing infrastructure, ensuring your home remains functional and efficient. With hot, humid summers common in Marietta, we also specialize in solutions like sump pumps and water-efficient fixtures to help homeowners manage moisture and conserve water.
serviceAreas.localContext: Marietta is known for its historic charm and established neighborhoods, many of which feature mid-century homes with original plumbing systems.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Plumbing is your trusted plumbing expert in Roswell, GA, a thriving community with approximately 95,000 residents. The city is home to upscale subdivisions built in the 1980s and 1990s, where modern plumbing systems are common but may still require maintenance or upgrades. Our team is well-versed in servicing high-end homes, offering everything from leak detection to water heater installations. Roswell's hot, humid summers make efficient plumbing systems essential, and we provide solutions like tankless water heaters and smart irrigation to help homeowners save on utility bills.
serviceAreas.localContext: Roswell is an affluent suburb known for its well-maintained neighborhoods and a mix of traditional and contemporary homes.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Plumbing delivers top-tier plumbing services to Alpharetta, GA, a bustling city with around 67,000 residents. Alpharetta is renowned for its upscale developments built in the 1990s and 2000s, featuring modern plumbing systems that occasionally need expert care. Whether you're dealing with a clogged drain or planning a bathroom remodel, our team provides reliable, professional service tailored to your needs. Given Alpharetta's hot, humid climate, we also recommend preventative maintenance to avoid issues like pipe corrosion or mold growth.
serviceAreas.localContext: Alpharetta is a fast-growing city with a reputation for luxury homes, excellent schools, and a thriving business community.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Plumbing is proud to serve Johns Creek, GA, an upscale city with approximately 84,000 residents. Many homes in Johns Creek were constructed in the 1990s and 2000s, featuring high-quality plumbing systems that still benefit from regular maintenance. Our team specializes in servicing these newer homes, offering everything from emergency repairs to custom installations. The hot, humid summers in Johns Creek can strain plumbing systems, so we provide solutions like water softeners and efficient fixtures to enhance durability and performance.
serviceAreas.localContext: Johns Creek is one of Georgia's most affluent suburbs, known for its spacious homes, top-rated schools, and well-planned communities.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Plumbing is your go-to plumbing service provider in Kennesaw, GA, a charming suburban city with around 35,000 residents. Kennesaw's housing stock primarily consists of 1980s and 1990s suburban developments, where plumbing systems may need updates or repairs. Our team is skilled in addressing common issues like pipe leaks, water pressure problems, and outdated fixtures. With Kennesaw's hot, humid summers, we also recommend seasonal inspections to prevent plumbing failures and ensure optimal system performance.
serviceAreas.localContext: Kennesaw offers a family-friendly atmosphere with a mix of mid-sized homes and newer developments, all within easy reach of Atlanta.

The city's hot, humid climate makes regular maintenance crucial, and we offer tailored solutions to keep your plumbing system running smoothly year-round.
serviceAreas.localContext: Sandy Springs combines urban convenience with suburban comfort, featuring a range of housing styles and a thriving commercial sector.

serviceAreas.localContext: Smyrna offers a lively mix of residential and commercial spaces, with easy access to Atlanta's amenities.
